critic_raw: The GNN engagement prediction experiment has three concerns: graph structural leakage from random node splits; the GNN vs. LR comparison implicitly tests graph structure value but should be explicitly framed with a node-features-only ablation; and the engagement outcome is not operationally defined.
all_issues_raised: Graph structural leakage from random node split — connected nodes in train/test allow message passing across boundary
GNN vs. LR comparison implicitly tests graph structure value but lacks explicit node-features-only ablation

round_transcripts.critic: Three issues: (1) Random split on graph data allows message passing across train/test boundary. (2) GNN vs. LR comparison needs explicit node-features-only ablation to isolate graph structure value. (3) Engagement outcome operationally undefined.
round_transcripts.defender: Transductive vs. inductive training mode is a critical omission — if transductive, graph leakage is real. If inductive, the held-out subgraph is isolated. The comparison design is valid but an ablation would strengthen it. Engagement definition is a documentation gap.

round_transcripts.critic: Transductive vs. inductive training specification is critical and unspecified. Engagement definition is a documentation gap.
round_transcripts.defender: Conceded: training mode must be specified; engagement outcome must be operationally defined. Core comparison is scientifically valid with these additions.

all_issues_adjudicated: Transductive vs. inductive GNN training not specified — determines whether graph structural leakage exists
